mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 52%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio over a 36-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation reduces real borrowing costs and preserves corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.0% quarter-over-quarter, strong macroeconomic demand supports corporate revenue projections and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal hit rate falls below 48% over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of synchronized economic recovery, increases in industrial production signal capacity utilization improvements that boost equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive 20-day returns during synchronized recovery periods, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 50% over a 24-month test window.
